evals.name: Asks for input when only given a vague description
evals.description: When the user describes a pipeline vaguely with no code, schema, or source details, the agent should ask for more information before producing documentation.
evals.prompt: Can you document our data pipeline?

rubric.pass_condition: Agent asks at least one clarifying question about the pipeline before producing documentation

evals.name: Produces all required output sections
evals.description: Given a clear pipeline description, the output should include Purpose, Data Sources, Pipeline Stages, Output Schema, and Known Limitations.
evals.prompt: Document this pipeline: We pull daily active user counts from our Postgres database, join them with campaign data from a CSV file, filter to users who opted in to communications, then write the result to a BigQuery table called `marketing.active_opted_in_users`. The output has fields: user_id (integer), campaign_id (string), opted_in (boolean), last_active_date (date).

rubric.pass_condition: All five required sections are present and non-empty

evals.name: Captures data quality issues when mentioned
evals.description: When the user mentions known data quality problems, the agent should include them in the Data Quality Notes section.
evals.prompt: Document this ETL. We pull from a legacy CRM that sometimes has duplicate records. We deduplicate on email address, but about 5% of emails are null so those records get dropped. Output goes to a reporting table.

rubric.pass_condition: Data Quality Notes section explicitly mentions duplicates, null emails, and the 5% drop rate

evals.name: Writes a plain-English overview readable by non-technical stakeholders
evals.description: The Purpose section should be understandable without technical knowledge — no SQL, no jargon.
evals.prompt: Document this: SELECT u.user_id, u.email, c.campaign_name FROM users u JOIN campaigns c ON u.campaign_id = c.id WHERE u.active = true AND u.consent = true INSERT INTO reporting.campaign_reach

rubric.pass_condition: The Purpose paragraph describes what the pipeline does in plain English without SQL syntax
